title: Revert "ValueTracking: Improve handling of fadd in computeKnownFPClass."
author: boomanaiden154
description:
Reverts llvm/llvm-project#174123

This caused test failures within LLVM libc. They can be reproduced by doing a libc build against a clang with this commit included and running `ninja -k 0 libc.test.src.math.smoke.log1p_test.__unit__ libc.test.src.math.smoke.log1p_test.__unit__.__NO_FMA_OPT`.

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body: I'm not really sure why this didn't show up in premerge. There's another failure that I'm seeing locally that requires MPFR to reproduce that I still need to bisect.

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
OK, so the explanation for it not failing for me is AArch64 host. This apparently doesn't handle enabling FTZ+DAZ, and the tests only handle x86/MXCSR. Unfortunately it still doesn't fail when I hack in enabling flushing for AArch64.

These two snippets look very relevant:
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/a9fee3127a6159ec6ca3ba1c7d1c329322985e20/libc/src/math/generic/log1p.cpp#L939

https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/a9fee3127a6159ec6ca3ba1c7d1c329322985e20/libc/src/math/generic/log1p.cpp#L954

This case should be hitting the second path

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body:
> hack in enabling flushing for AArch64.

Which won't help anyway since it doesn't have the separate input and output mode

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body: But also what this test is doing is illegal. You can't simply flip the control mode mid-non-strictfp function. This should be compiling these functions with the correct denormal-fp-math flag/attribute

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body: I think I have the full picture now. TLDR; this patch is right and libc is wrong. The problematic sequence is `return (x + x == 0.0) ? x + x : x;` Here is the extracted bitcode: [libc_log1p.bc.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24421536/libc_log1p.bc.zip) (for aarch64, but the target doesn't matter other than for execution context). I see the diff with the patch, and it does look like the [second zero check](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/a9fee3127a6159ec6ca3ba1c7d1c329322985e20/libc/src/math/generic/log1p.cpp#L954) was removed . The [dominating condition](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/a9fee3127a6159ec6ca3ba1c7d1c329322985e20/libc/src/math/generic/log1p.cpp#L938) ensures x as an input must be treated as 0 if the input mode is known IEEE. We make this assumption [here](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/2eb8ee137f28e46a1711a28b6575083dafba7c8e/llvm/include/llvm/IR/GenericFloatingPointPredicateUtils.h#L146), which assumes not-DAZ inputs (which is the case in the failing FTZ-only example). The [later check](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/a9fee3127a6159ec6ca3ba1c7d1c329322985e20/libc/src/math/generic/log1p.cpp#L954), since x is known nonzero, x + x is also not 0, and thus this directly returns the uncanonicalized input x. In this test case that is the minimum denormal 0x1p-1074, which is the failing value instead of the expected exact 0. So this test is asserting the result for a non-canonical output value, but didn't canonicalize the output. We rely on the fact that the output mode will not flush a denormal [here](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/5cbc6a63a19955b5f6fd3f62da8673893abaef9d/llvm/lib/Analysis/ValueTracking.cpp#L5666). But the execution mode is wrong given the illegal mode switch in the test, combined with the fact the library function was compiled with the assumption of IEEE handling. If the code wants to support all denormal modes, it needs to be compiled with denormal-fp-math=dynamic

comments.author: arsenm
comments.body: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174332 re-lands the easy part until libc is fixed
